steps.title: Find the exception
steps.content: The first sentence gives the general pattern: most discovered exoplanets orbit G-type stars like the Sun. The next sentence introduces KELT-9b, which orbits a B-type star that is hotter and more massive than the Sun.

steps.title: Signal contrast
steps.content: Choice A signals similarity, Choice C signals result, and Choice D would make KELT-9b an example of the G-type pattern. Because the second sentence presents an exception to that pattern, a contrast transition is needed. The answer is B.
